    "R1.4": "The text mentions one prior work (not named) and articulates a delta compared to always-using large models.",
    "R1.7": "There are no explicit citations in the text, but it does mention comparing small and large model rollouts against ground truth success.",
    "RX.1": "The text discusses uncertainty by highlighting cases where the classifier sends hard steps to the smaller model, which incur task-level costs.",
    "RX.3": "The text states that the evaluation tasks are drawn from the same task family as the training set, suggesting potential overlap.",
    "RX.4": "The text does not introduce new errors or harmful content.",
    "RX.5": "The text mentions releasing the trained classifier weights and routing infrastructure, but it doesn't provide details on how these changes are traced."

  "confidence": {"R1.4": 0.7, "R1.7": 0.6, "RX.1": 0.8, "RX.3": 0.5, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.6},

rationale.R1.4: The text mentions one prior work (not named) and articulates a delta compared to always-using large models.
rationale.R1.7: There are no explicit citations in the text, but it does mention comparing small and large model rollouts against ground truth success.
rationale.RX.1: The text discusses uncertainty by highlighting cases where the classifier sends hard steps to the smaller model, which incur task-level costs.
rationale.RX.3: The text states that the evaluation tasks are drawn from the same task family as the training set, suggesting potential overlap.
rationale.RX.4: The text does not introduce new errors or harmful content.
rationale.RX.5: The text mentions releasing the trained classifier weights and routing infrastructure, but it doesn't provide details on how these changes are traced.
